meta.name: EDE-QS Eating Disorder Examination Questionnaire Short
meta.fullName: Eating Disorder Examination Questionnaire - Short Form
meta.description: The EDE-QS is a 12-item short form of the Eating Disorder Examination Questionnaire (EDE-Q), designed to provide a brief yet reliable assessment of eating disorder psychopathology. A score of 15 or above suggests possible eating disorder concerns warranting further evaluation.
meta.author: Gideon et al. (2016)
meta.reference: Gideon N, Hawkes N, Mond J, et al. Development and psychometric validation of the EDE-QS, a 12 item short form of the Eating Disorder Examination Questionnaire (EDE-Q). PLoS ONE. 2016;11(5):e0152744.

questions.text: Have you been deliberately trying to limit the amount of food you eat to influence your shape or weight?
Have you gone for long periods of time (8 hours or more) without eating anything in order to influence your shape or weight?
Has thinking about food or its calorie content made it much more difficult to concentrate on things you are interested in?
Has thinking about shape or weight made it much more difficult to concentrate on things you are interested in?
Have you had a definite fear that you might gain weight or become fat?
Have you had a strong desire to lose weight?
Have you tried to control your weight or shape by making yourself sick or taking laxatives?
Have you exercised in a driven or compulsive way as a means of controlling your weight, shape or amount of fat?
Have you had a sense of having lost control over your eating?
On how many of the past 7 days have you eaten in secret?
How dissatisfied have you been with your weight?
How dissatisfied have you been with your shape?

ranges.label: No Concern
ranges.description: Your responses do not indicate significant eating disorder concerns. Your relationship with food and body image appears to be within a healthy range.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy and balanced relationship with food
Practice body appreciation and gratitude for what your body can do
Be mindful of societal pressures regarding body image

ranges.label: Mild Concern
ranges.description: Your responses suggest some mild concerns related to eating, weight, or body shape. While these may not indicate a clinical eating disorder, they are worth paying attention to.
suggestions: Reflect on your attitudes toward food and your body
Avoid restrictive dieting and focus on balanced nutrition
Consider speaking with a healthcare professional if concerns persist

ranges.label: Moderate Concern
ranges.description: Your score is above the clinical cutoff, suggesting possible eating disorder psychopathology. These concerns may be affecting your daily life and well-being.
suggestions: Consider seeking a professional evaluation for eating concerns
Speak with a healthcare provider about your eating patterns
Look into evidence-based treatments for eating difficulties
Remember that eating disorders are treatable and recovery is possible

ranges.label: High Concern
ranges.description: Your responses indicate significant eating disorder concerns that are likely impacting your physical and psychological health. Professional evaluation is strongly recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional evaluation as soon as possible
Contact a specialist in eating disorders for comprehensive assessment
Eating disorders are serious but treatable conditions
Support from specialized professionals can make a significant difference
Consider reaching out to an eating disorder helpline for immediate guidance
